key_findings.finding: init.ts contains ~170 lines of dead code (7 unused helper functions) that duplicate the plan/apply pattern. Indicates an incomplete refactor — the older imperative helpers were left behind after the build/apply split.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Delete L1383-1554 (prepareFreshPath, prepareWritableDirectory, writeNewFile, copyTemplateIfMissing, copyExecutableTemplateIfMissing, writeCodexHooksConfig, mergeClaudeStopHook) — none are referenced outside init.ts. Adopt knip in CI to make this regression-proof. Saves ~9% of file size and removes a divergent code path that could be reintroduced by mistake.

key_findings.finding: MCP bootstrap README resource handler lets fs.readFile ENOENT bubble unwrapped to the MCP client. With Fabric not initialized (.fabric/bootstrap/README.md missing), the client receives a Node ENOENT stack instead of a structured MCP error — degrading discoverability.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Wrap readFile in try/catch; on ENOENT throw a structured error like McpError(NotFound, 'Fabric is not initialized: run `fab init`') — matches MCP 2025-11-25 error semantics and gives clients a reliable signal.

key_findings.finding: events.jsonl is written with raw appendFile (no fsync, no rotation, no lock). On fsyncgate-class filesystems a partial write corrupts the ledger; doctor's parseable check (services/doctor.ts:431) detects but does not recover. Writer is non-atomic while peer writers in _shared.ts use atomicWriteText.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) accept appendFile semantics + add doctor --fix that truncates ledger to last complete line, OR (b) introduce a single-writer queue (e.g. atomically.appendFile) with explicit fsync handling and ledger rotation on EIO. Document the 'snapshot=atomic, ledger=append-only-best-effort' contract in _shared.ts.

key_findings.finding: normalizeApiError uses error.message string-prefix matching to map 3 specific errors to HTTP 403/404. Any reword of those strings in a service file silently downgrades 403/404 → 500. Zero tests cover the matching branches.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Introduce typed error classes (PathOutsideRootError, HumanLockEntryNotFoundError, LedgerEntryNotFoundError) — already pattern-precedented by AgentsMetaFileMissingError at L76. Replace string-prefix branches with instanceof checks. Add unit tests for each branch.

key_findings.finding: writeDefaultBootstrap (doctor --fix for bootstrap_missing) writes a hardcoded one-line README, discarding framework variant content that buildFabricBootstrapGuide would produce. So `fab init` and `fab doctor --fix` produce divergent bootstrap files.
key_findings.suggested_fix: writeDefaultBootstrap should call buildFabricBootstrapGuide(projectRoot) (move the helper into shared/server, or have CLI hand a generator into runDoctorFix). Otherwise running `fab doctor --fix` after manual ledger removal silently downgrades the user's project to a stub README.

key_findings.finding: tryBuildRuleMeta swallows ALL errors and returns null. When buildRuleMeta fails (invalid markdown, bad zod, unreadable file), inspectMeta and inspectRuleTestIndex degrade silently — staleness check is skipped, doctor reports 'present:false' or 'stale:false' and the user has no actionable signal.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Capture the error message and surface it as an inspection.warning string (not just dropped to null). Add a `meta_build_failed` doctor warning code so users see 'agents.meta build skipped: <reason>' instead of phantom-fresh state.

key_findings.finding: parseLedgerAppendedEvent / parseEventLedgerAppendedEvent / parseEventLedgerLine all swallow JSON parse + zod validation failures and return null. Invalid lines are silently dropped on read. No telemetry. Doctor's parseable check is the only safety net but it does not surface line numbers.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Parse functions should accept a logger callback (or push to an out-parameter array of warnings); doctor.inspectEventLedger should report line numbers + reason for each invalid line, not just 'contains an invalid JSON line'. Consider an LRU-warned set so repeat reads do not log-spam.

key_findings.finding: buildClaudeSettingsWritePlan reads JSON, validates, but stores the raw `parsed` value into the plan as `ClaudeSettings` (an unsafe cast). settings.hooks could be any record shape; the second-tier isRecord check (L957) only protects against array/string/null, not against nested type drift.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Define a small zod schema for the subset of ~/.claude/settings.json that init writes (only hooks.Stop matters). z.object({ hooks: z.object({ Stop: z.array(z.unknown()).optional() }).passthrough().optional() }).passthrough() — preserves unknown keys but typechecks the path Fabric mutates.

key_findings.finding: writeJsonAtomically (init.ts L1556) uses pid in tmpName but does not call fsync. On crash mid-rename the user is left with either old or new file (acceptable), but on fsync failures the rename is reported as success while the data is lost. Used for codex hooks.json + claude settings.json.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Replace with `atomically` package or add explicit fd.sync() before rename. Low-priority because these are seldom-written init artifacts, not hot paths.

key_findings.finding: MCP server (packages/server/src/index.ts) registers no SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers. Claude Code issue #15945 documents 70+ zombie server processes from this exact pattern. startStdioServer awaits server.connect and never installs cleanup.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Install signal handlers in startStdioServer that call transport.close() then process.exit. Add a deadline-exit (e.g. 5s) so a hung close doesn't keep the process alive. Verify in test by sending SIGTERM to a child stdio server.

key_findings.finding: MCP tools registered without readOnlyHint/idempotentHint annotations. fab_get_rule_sections + fab_plan_context are read-only but clients (especially Cursor with 40-tool ceiling) cannot auto-approve them, costing UX and tool-budget room.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Pass `annotations: { readOnlyHint: true, idempotentHint: true }` in registerTool options. Per MCP 2025-03-26 spec; supported by all 3 in-scope clients.

key_findings.finding: readPackageName silently swallows all errors → returns undefined. An invalid package.json (BOM, trailing comma, etc.) is treated as 'project has no name' and bootstrap-guide.ts L25 falls back to parse(workspaceRoot).base. User sees no signal that their package.json is broken.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Distinguish 'missing' vs 'parse error'. On parse failure write a stderr warning via t() so the user knows the bootstrap was templated against a directory name fallback. Keep existing fallback behavior.

key_findings.finding: config-install.test.ts L23-25 hard-codes the retired Gemini alias. Will become a regression-blocker the moment gemini is removed from parseClientFilter. This is the ONLY direct test of parseClientFilter.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Replace with `parseClientFilter('cursor,codex')` → Set(['Cursor','CodexCLI']). Add a contract test that asserts the full ClientKind enum equals exactly ['ClaudeCodeCLI','ClaudeCodeDesktop','Cursor','CodexCLI'] — single-line guard against client-scope drift.

key_findings.finding: No test exists for normalizeApiError despite it gating all HTTP status codes. _error.ts is a single file with 4 branches × 3 string-prefix cases — untested.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Add packages/server/src/api/_error.test.ts: one test per branch (status+code mapping), plus a boundary test asserting 'INTERNAL_ERROR'/500 fallback for unknown errors. ~30 lines for full coverage.

key_findings.finding: No CLI command test for fab/bootstrap.ts. installBootstrap is exercised only as a side-effect of init-claude-install.test.ts. parseClientFilter (private), mapClientKind, mapBootstrapClientToClientKind have zero direct coverage.
key_findings.suggested_fix: Add packages/cli/__tests__/bootstrap.test.ts covering mapClientKind/mapBootstrapClientToClientKind exhaustiveness, plus parseClientFilter for unknown clients (should error or filter).

key_findings.finding: No test covers the partial-line corruption recovery path in event-ledger. The existing 'tolerates malformed lines' test (event-ledger.test.ts:62) only covers '{not json' (full malformed line); it does NOT cover an actual mid-line truncation (e.g. '{"kind":"fabric-event' without closing brace, or an interleaved fragment).
key_findings.suggested_fix: Add a test that writes 'valid-line\n{"kind":"fab' (trailing partial) and asserts readEventLedger() returns [valid-line, ...]. Once doctor --fix learns ledger truncation, also test that path.

code_anchors.significance: Catch arm has no error binding and no log. JSON parse errors and zod validation failures are indistinguishable to the caller. Diagnosing 'why is my event missing from the SSE stream?' requires source-side instrumentation we do not have.

Raw appendFile: no fsync, no rotation, no per-process lock. Two writers (CLI doctor + MCP server) race on the same file. Mixed durability with atomicWriteText elsewhere. Fsyncgate makes EIO unrecoverable but here we wouldn't even know.

All callers (inspectMeta L324, inspectRuleTestIndex L469) treat null as 'no built revision' — masking schema violations, IO errors, malformed rule files. Doctor reports a clean state when the build chain is broken.

Brittle string-prefix coupling. The neighbor branches (L76, L84) use proper instanceof + error.code — these 3 string branches are an inconsistent legacy island. lint-protected-tokens does NOT cover these strings.

If .fabric/bootstrap/README.md is missing, raw ENOENT propagates to the MCP transport. Client sees a Node error name, not 'NotFound: run fab init'. Common when the user installs the MCP server before running init.

Doctor's auto-fix for bootstrap_missing writes a 70-byte stub. fab init writes a framework-aware README via buildFabricBootstrapGuide() with project name + framework variant. So `fab doctor --fix` silently downgrades the project. The names match a layering issue: bootstrap-guide lives in CLI but doctor lives in server.

Seven helpers, ~170 lines, ZERO call sites. The plan/apply pattern (L842-1008) replaced them but the originals were never deleted. tsc with strict + noUnusedLocals would catch this; current tsconfig must allow unused functions. knip would flag immediately.

code_anchors.snippet:
steps: [
      { name: "preflight" },
      { name: "scaffold" },
      ...stages.map((stage) => ({ name: stage.name, skipped: stage.skipped }) as InitExecutionStep),
      { name: "post-setup" },
    ],
code_anchors.significance: `as InitExecutionStep` cast — needed because TS cannot infer that stage.name is a discriminant. Removable by typing stages with the discriminated union directly: `stages: InitStagePlan[]` already exists; the cast is masking an inferable structure.

Two-step cast: parsed:unknown → isRecord guard → cast to ClaudeSettings. Justified for the outer record shape, but ClaudeSettings has a typed hooks structure that is NOT validated. Could lose data if settings.hooks.Stop[].hooks[].command is wrong shape.

Cast `as ClaudeStopHookEntry[]` because nextStopHooks was typed as `unknown[]`. Real shape comes from prior validation but TS cannot see it. Justified-with-comment level if narrowed via a type predicate; currently uncommented.

Only top-level .then() in the codebase, and it IS paired with a .catch (verified by reading context). Not a floating promise. CLI's commands/index.ts L2-5 also use .then() but inside arrow functions that are awaited downstream — also safe. Promise hygiene overall is clean.

Top-level entry point has a catch but no SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers, so the stdio server can outlive its parent (Claude Code issue #15945). The `.catch` only fires for the connect promise rejection, not signal-driven shutdown.

chain: runInitCommand → resolveInitCliIntent → buildInitExecutionPlan → buildInitFabricPlan → buildFabricBootstrapGuide → executeInitFabricPlan → executeInitStagePlan(bootstrap/mcp/hooks)
runDoctorFix → writeDefaultBootstrap (writes 70-byte stub) — DIVERGES from `fab init` which calls buildFabricBootstrapGuide for framework-aware content
MCP transport → registerResource handler → readFile(.fabric/bootstrap/README.md) → ENOENT → unwrapped to client
service throws Error → sendUnknownError → normalizeApiError → string-prefix match → status mapping
appendEventLedgerEvent → eventLedgerEventSchema.parse → ensureParentDirectory → appendFile (NOT atomic, no fsync)

test_gap_matrix.consequential_gap: normalizeApiError L93-115 has 3 string-prefix branches (403/404 mappings) plus 4 instanceof branches and 1 fallback. Zero coverage — any rewording elsewhere silently degrades to 500.

test_gap_matrix.test_file: (none — covered only as side-effect of init-claude-install.test.ts L66-74)
test_gap_matrix.consequential_gap: mapClientKind (L181) and mapBootstrapClientToClientKind (L201) switch tables — no exhaustiveness/regression test. parseClientFilter for unknown clients untested.

resolveClients (L70-89) and detectClientSupports (L101-214) are the dispatch core for the entire client-scope refactor. Coverage is only via init-mcp-scope side effects. The DetectedClientSupport contract is not asserted anywhere.
L62 'tolerates malformed lines' covers FULL invalid lines but not TRAILING PARTIAL (the actual fsyncgate failure mode). No concurrency test (two writers). No fsync-error simulation.
L73 asserts only the registered tool names; no test exercises the resource handler at L67-87, so ENOENT-when-uninitialized is undefined behavior. No SIGTERM/SIGINT handler test.

9 test files exist, but none target the orchestrator seams (buildInitExecutionPlan, executeInitStagePlan, the failure paths at L820-823 that swallow stage errors as 'failed' disposition without surfacing the cause). 1897 lines + many private helpers + dead code (L1383-1554) implies high refactor risk.
ClaudeCodeCLIWriter (L92) and CursorWriter (L112) — the two surviving JSON writers — have NO direct unit tests. Snapshot-test gap noted in research.json codebase_gaps.

proposed_modules.name: init/cli.ts
proposed_modules.responsibility: Citty command definition, arg parsing, intent resolution, top-level orchestration. Keeps `runInitCommand`, `resolveInitCliIntent`, `initCommand` definition. ~300 lines.

proposed_modules.name: init/plan.ts
proposed_modules.responsibility: Pure plan construction + execution dispatch. `buildInitExecutionPlan`, `executeInitExecutionPlan`, `buildInitFabricPlan`, `executeInitFabricPlan`, `buildPlanOnlyScaffoldResult`, related types (InitScaffoldPlan, InitExecutionPlan). ~370 lines. This is the testable core — extract first.

proposed_modules.name: init/stages.ts
proposed_modules.responsibility: Per-stage execution: `executeInitStagePlan`, the 3 build*Plan/apply*Plan pairs (codex hooks, claude settings, optional templates), `installLocalFabricServer`. ~170 lines. Calls into bootstrap/config/hooks modules.

proposed_modules.name: init/wizard.ts
proposed_modules.responsibility: Interactive prompts: `createDefaultInitWizardAdapter`, `confirmInGroup`, `selectMcpInstallModeInGroup`, `collectLockedWizardStages`, banner/badge formatters. ~200 lines. Already isolated by InitWizardAdapter interface — clean extraction.

proposed_modules.name: init/render.ts
proposed_modules.responsibility: Pure presentation: `printInitScaffoldResult`, `printInitPostSetup`, `printInitPlanPreview`, `printInitPlanSummary`, `printInitCapabilitySummary`, `formatInit*` helpers, label functions. ~400 lines. No state, easy to unit test.

proposed_modules.name: init/taxonomy.ts
proposed_modules.responsibility: Taxonomy markdown generation: `createInitialMeta`, `buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown`, `formatInitialL1Buckets`, `formatInitialL2Signals`, `sanitizeTaxonomyLabel`. ~90 lines, pure functions, ideal for unit testing.

proposed_modules.name: DELETE
proposed_modules.responsibility: Dead code — 7 unused legacy helpers (prepareFreshPath, prepareWritableDirectory, writeNewFile, copyTemplateIfMissing, copyExecutableTemplateIfMissing, writeCodexHooksConfig, mergeClaudeStopHook). Confirmed zero callers across packages/. ~170 lines saved.

type_safety_audit.suggestion: Type stages with the discriminated union from the start so map() returns InitExecutionStep[] without cast. Removable.
Replace with a zod schema for the subset Fabric mutates (hooks.Stop). Keep .passthrough() to preserve unknown user fields.
Justified — preceded by isRecord(settings.hooks) check at L957. Could be tightened with a type predicate so cast disappears, but current form is defensible.
Push a typed value (satisfies ClaudeStopHookEntry) into nextStopHooks at L988 so it's already typed. Removes the cast.
Function is dead code (see init_decomposition.DELETE). Resolve by deleting L1383-L1554.
Justified — explicitly downcasts to unknown so the next line MUST validate. Idiomatic safe pattern.
Justified — parsed value is immediately handed to safeParse. The cast only appeases TS for the spread on L78.
Justified — same pattern: cast then validate. Could be replaced by `JSON.parse(line) satisfies unknown` if TS is happy with property access on unknown after kind narrow.
Same idiomatic pattern as event-ledger.ts — kept.
Justified — empty-object initializer for a Record being progressively filled. Standard TS idiom.

type_safety_audit.construct: window as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
type_safety_audit.suggestion: Justified — runtime-introspection of window globals (e.g. Vite injection). Double-cast is the standard escape hatch.
Justified — preceded by zod success branch where value is known to be the parsed object.

questions_for_user: init.ts dead code: confirm I should delete L1383-L1554 in this stabilization sweep, or do you want a separate cleanup PR after the client-scope removal lands?

writeDefaultBootstrap divergence (doctor stub vs init framework-aware): is the right fix to (a) hoist buildFabricBootstrapGuide into shared and have doctor call it, or (b) emit a manual-fix issue + delete writeDefaultBootstrap entirely so the only path to a real bootstrap is `fab init`?

events.jsonl durability: prefer (a) atomically.appendFile + fsync + ledger rotation on EIO, or (b) document append-best-effort + add doctor truncate-to-last-valid-line --fix? Option (b) is smaller scope.

_error.ts string-prefix matching: replace with typed errors (PathOutsideRootError etc.) — okay to introduce these classes in services/ now, or keep error-class proliferation off-limits in v1.7.0 patch scope?

MCP server signal handlers: should startStdioServer install SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP cleanup in this release, or defer? Risk is real (Claude Code issue #15945 zombie processes) but adds runtime behavior change.

init.ts decomposition: split now or after the client-scope trim? Splitting first means the trim PR touches an already-extracted plan.ts (smaller diff); splitting after means stabilization stays purely subtractive.
Tool annotations on MCP tools (readOnlyHint/idempotentHint): include in v1.7.0 stabilization or post-release? Pure addition, no behavior change.

_metadata.perspective_focus: code-level details, smells, bugs, test gaps
_metadata.method: Per-finding file read at cited line ranges (30-50 lines context); validated each Layer-1 smell against actual code; cross-checked test coverage by reading every relevant *.test.ts; ran multi-pattern grep for promise hygiene + type assertions; verified dead-code claim by grepping all callers across packages/.
key_discoveries_beyond_layer1: init.ts has ~170 lines of dead code (7 unused helpers L1383-1554) — not previously flagged
writeDefaultBootstrap doctor-fix DIVERGES from fab init — silent downgrade vector
MCP server has zero signal handlers — concrete v1.7.0 stability gap
Promise hygiene is actually clean (only safe .then chains found)
type assertions audit: most are justified parse-then-validate; ~4 in init.ts are removable
